entities.name: Button
entities.description: A clickable button for triggering actions.

agentDocumentBlocks.purpose: Trigger a user-initiated action within the current view without causing page navigation.

items.description: Use button for in-page actions; use link for navigation to a URL.
Use button when a visible text label is present; use icon-button for icon-only affordances.

items.guidance: Do not use for navigating to a different page or URL.
items.rationale: Buttons signal actions and links signal navigation. Assistive technology announces each differently, and users rely on that distinction to predict what a control will do.

items.guidance: Limit each surface to one primary-emphasis button.
items.rationale: A single primary action keeps the visual hierarchy clear. When several buttons compete at primary emphasis, the intended next step becomes ambiguous.

items.guidance: Always provide an accessible label via visible text or aria-label.
items.rationale: Icon-only buttons without an accessible name are unusable with screen readers and fail WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

items.guidance: Using a button to navigate to another page.
items.rationale: Instead: Use a link element with href.

items.guidance: Placing multiple primary buttons on the same surface.
items.rationale: Instead: Use one primary and one or more secondary or tertiary buttons.

items.description: When the user needs to trigger an action such as submitting a form, saving data, or opening a dialog.
When the action navigates to a different page or URL — use a link instead, which carries native navigation semantics.

items.title: Intent
items.body: Define and organize a set of related design tokens, providing shared defaults for type and category.

items.guidance: Do not reference base palette tokens directly in component code.
items.rationale: Always reference semantic tokens that point to palette values.

items.title: Intent
items.body: Define and organize a set of related design tokens, providing shared defaults for type and category.

items.guidance: Do not reference base palette tokens directly in component code.
items.rationale: Always reference semantic tokens that point to palette values.

entities.name: Dark Mode
entities.description: Dark color mode for low-light environments.

overrides.description: Muted text for secondary content in dark mode.

agentDocumentBlocks.purpose: Apply a dark color palette for low-light environments and user preference, maintaining WCAG AA contrast throughout.

items.description: Use dark for low-light comfort; use high-contrast for users with low vision requiring maximum contrast.

items.guidance: Always apply at the application root, not on individual components.
items.rationale: Scoping the theme to the root keeps every surface consistent. Per-component theming produces mixed-mode screens and doubles the testing surface.

items.guidance: Do not mix dark-mode and light-mode semantic tokens on the same surface.
items.rationale: Semantic tokens are tuned for contrast within one mode. Mixing modes breaks those tuned pairings unpredictably.

items.guidance: Applying dark theme only to part of a page while the rest remains light.
items.rationale: Instead: Use inline theme switching for isolated components; apply the theme at the root for global coverage.

entities.name: Color
entities.description: The color system governing palette usage, semantic mapping, and accessibility.

agentDocumentBlocks.purpose: Document semantic color tokens and enforce their consistent, accessible application across surfaces, text, and interactive states.

items.description: Color governs palette and semantic role mapping; theme entities govern how the palette shifts per mode.

items.guidance: Do not hard-code hex values; reference semantic color tokens instead.
items.rationale: Hard-coded values escape theming, dark mode, and audits — the palette can no longer change in one place.

items.guidance: Maintain WCAG AA contrast for text and interactive elements on every surface.
items.rationale: AA is the system's compliance floor: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text and interactive components.

entities.name: Form Validation
entities.description: Validates user input and communicates errors on form submission.

agentDocumentBlocks.purpose: Guide multi-component composition for surfacing, communicating, and resolving form validation errors.

items.description: Use error-messaging for validation and system errors; use empty-state for the absence of content.
Use error-messaging for form validation flows; use alert for non-form system or status messages.

items.guidance: Always include both an inline field error and a summary when multiple fields fail.
items.rationale: The summary gives screen reader users one announcement point and an error count; inline errors give sighted users fix-it-here proximity. Each alone strands one audience.

items.guidance: Do not use toast notifications as the sole error communication for form validation.
items.rationale: Toasts dismiss themselves and are not tied to the failing field. Once gone, the user has no path back to what went wrong.

items.guidance: Showing errors only on submit without inline field feedback.
items.rationale: Instead: Show inline errors on field blur and in a summary on submit.

items.description: When a form submission can fail per field and the user must fix specific inputs before continuing.
